title: RecommendReport
description:
Envelope emitted by `aegis-boot recommend --json`. Untagged wrapper around three mutually-exclusive shapes: a full catalog listing, a single-entry response, or a miss. Consumers branch on the presence of `entries` / `entry` / `error`.

The miss shape intentionally omits `tool_version` — matches the existing wire format. Future schema bumps can unify the three shapes; Phase 4b-6 preserves the current contract.

anyOf.description: Full catalog listing. Emitted when `aegis-boot recommend --json` is called with no slug.
Single-entry response. Emitted when the slug matched one catalog entry exactly.
Miss — the slug didn't match any entry.

count.description: Total entries in the catalog. Equals `entries.len()`.

entries.description: All catalog entries in the order `CATALOG` defines them (typically alphabetical by slug).

tool_version.description: `aegis-boot` binary version that produced this envelope.

RecommendEntry.description: One curated catalog entry. Used in both [`RecommendCatalogReport::entries`] and [`RecommendSingleReport::entry`].

iso_url.description: HTTPS URL of the ISO body.

purpose.description: One-line operator-facing purpose (e.g. `"Standard server install media"`).

sb.description: Secure Boot status string — one of `"signed:<vendor>"` (e.g. `"signed:canonical"`), `"unsigned-needs-mok"`, or `"unknown"`.

sha256_url.description: HTTPS URL of the upstream SHA256SUMS file.

sig_url.description: HTTPS URL of the detached signature over the SHA256SUMS file (typically a GPG `.gpg`).

size_mib.description: ISO size in mebibytes (rounded to nearest; informational for download-time estimates, not a strict guarantee). `u32` accommodates up to ~4 PiB — plenty of headroom for any realistic ISO.

slug.description: Short stable identifier (e.g. `"ubuntu-24.04-live-server"`). Used by `aegis-boot fetch <slug>` to resolve a URL set.

RecommendMissReport.description: Miss variant of [`RecommendReport`] — no catalog entry matched the given slug. The envelope is deliberately asymmetric from the success variants (no `tool_version`) to match the existing wire format; tightening to always emit `tool_version` would be an additive (non-breaking) future change.

tool_version.description: `aegis-boot` binary version that produced this envelope.
